nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as fully autonomous task completer
nodes.grounding_span: I would keep the conversation with AI going until I have a template that I like

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary for tasks with existing efficient systems
nodes.grounding_span: it saved me the time that I would usually spend to think about how to word things

nodes.label: AI as motivational and anxiety-reducing companion
nodes.label_negative: AI as independent template generator without collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: I would want to collaborate more so that it could provide tips on how to improve soft skills and ease anxiety

nodes.label: AI use sanctioned by workplace
nodes.label_negative: AI use as accepted and encouraged
nodes.grounding_span: the overall workplace culture at the rehab hospital I'm at is that AI is a more taboo item to use

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: it is a tool that I can work with collaboratively in order to improve my work quality and efficiency

nodes.label: AI use in patient care documentation
nodes.grounding_span: we would be actively speaking into our phones/a mic during patient sessions about what is going on

nodes.label: Professional efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: it saved me the time that I would usually spend to think about how to word things

nodes.label: Patient confidentiality and privacy
nodes.grounding_span: leave out patient identifying information due to privacy reasons

nodes.label: Skill preservation and professional adaptability
nodes.grounding_span: I still actively complete these tasks without the use of AI over 80% of the time

nodes.label: Patient-centered care
nodes.grounding_span: it would be great if some of the time charting could be used seeing more patients instead

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: A time that AI made me feel particularly satisfied was when I was asked by a physician for an update via email when it was 3:55 pm

nodes.label: Hesitancy overcome by colleague influence
nodes.grounding_span: It also shaped my approach from being more hesitant at first to use it to more open to it

nodes.label: Agreement with workplace taboo against mindless AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I agree that it should not be something that one uses mindlessly in order to avoid doing a work task

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for documentation
nodes.grounding_span: I could envision the relationship changing to where we would be actively speaking into our phones/a mic

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I would keep the conversation with AI going until I have a template that I like

nodes.label: Privacy-conscious compartmentalization — separating AI use from identifiable data
nodes.grounding_span: leave out patient identifying information due to privacy reasons
